title: [macOS] recovering `podman-machine-default_fedora-coreos-38.20230625.2.0-qemu.x86_64.qcow2`
body:
### Issue Description

I `rm -r ~/.local/share/containers/podman` to temporarily save storage. Now I want to use `podman` again. Is there a way for `podman` to reinstall the VM?

It looks like `podman` thinks the VM still exists (but it doesn't).

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `brew install podman-desktop`
2. `rm -r ~/.local/share/containers/podman`
3. `brew uninstall --zap podman-desktop`
4. `brew install podman-desktop`
5. `open -a 'podman desktop'`
6. `podman run --rm -it ubuntu`


### Describe the results you received

```console
$ podman run --rm -it ubuntu
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:49495: connect: connection refused
$ podman system connection list
Name                         URI                                                         Identity                                    Default
podman-machine-default       ssh://core@127.0.0.1:49495/run/user/501/podman/podman.sock  /Users/dracula/.ssh/podman-machine-default  true
podman-machine-default-root  ssh://root@127.0.0.1:49495/run/podman/podman.sock           /Users/dracula/.ssh/podman-machine-default  false
$ podman machine init
Error: podman-machine-default: VM already exists
$ podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Waiting for VM ...
Error: qemu exited unexpectedly with exit code 1, stderr: qemu-system-x86_64: -drive if=virtio,file=/Users/dracula/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman-machine-default_fedora-coreos-38.20230625.2.0-qemu.x86_64.qcow2: Could not open '/Users/dracula/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman-machine-default_fedora-coreos-38.20230625.2.0-qemu.x86_64.qcow2': No such file or directory

$ file ~/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman-machine-default_fedora-coreos-38.20230625.2.0-qemu.x86_64.qcow2
/Users/dracula/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman-machine-default_fedora-coreos-38.20230625.2.0-qemu.x86_64.qcow2: cannot open `/Users/dracula/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman-machine-default_fedora-coreos-38.20230625.2.0-qemu.x86_64.qcow2' (No such file or directory)
$ find ~/.local/share/containers/podman                                                        
/Users/dracula/.local/share/containers/podman
/Users/dracula/.local/share/containers/podman/machine
/Users/dracula/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/podman.sock
/Users/dracula/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu
```

### Describe the results you expected

`podman --rm -it ubuntu` starts an interactive shell in an `ubuntu` container.

### podman info output

```yaml
$ podman version
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:49495: connect: connection refused
$ podman info
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:49495: connect: connection refused
$ podman --version
podman version 4.6.0
$ brew install podman-desktop
Warning: Not upgrading podman-desktop, the latest version is already installed
$ brew install podman
Warning: podman 4.6.0 is already installed and up-to-date.
To reinstall 4.6.0, run:
  brew reinstall podman
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

    macOS: 13.5-x86_64

### Additional information

Issue happens after manually deleting the VM.

comments.body: I don't think there's any way to recover after removing _internal_ data.  In your specific case, you may need to remove `~/.config/containers/podman/machine/qemu` to stop Podman from thinking the VM is still there.

Thx! `rm -rvf ~/.config/containers/podman/machine/qemu` did it.

Note: I could not find documentation to fully uninstall `podman` (including the VM). Running `brew uninstall podman` keeps the artifacts (as expected). The closest I could find is some suggestions in the following issue:

- #11319

(It included `podman machine rm podman-machine-default` which fulfills my use case)
